prompt: JOBS-TO-BE-DONE + OFF-THE-SHELF. For kolm (signed, verifiable security evidence about the AI a company ships, to clear enterprise security review in days not weeks), what is the JOB the buyer is hiring it for, in their language? Is it 'help me pass the security review', 'help me answer the AI security questionnaire', 'help me prove our AI is safe to customers', 'help me close enterprise deals faster', 'help me look enterprise-ready'? What would make this SELL OFF THE SHELF - i.e. a builder lands on the homepage and within 5 seconds thinks 'yes that is exactly my problem, let me try the free scan'? Identify the single sharpest JOB statement, the trigger moment they search for help, and the exact search terms / phrases they would type. Cite real 2026 examples of products that nail self-serve 'pass the security review / answer the questionnaire' positioning (Vanta Questionnaire Automation, SafeBase, security trust centers, etc., with URLs).
content:
**The single sharpest JOB statement (in buyer language):** “Help me pass the AI security review / questionnaire with signed, verifiable evidence so enterprise buyers approve us in days, not weeks.”

This is the core “job” buyers hire kolm for. It directly addresses the painful, deal-blocking step in enterprise sales where security/compliance teams demand proof about the AI component (model security, data handling, inference risks, attestations, etc.). Other phrasings like “help me prove our AI is safe to customers” or “help me close enterprise deals faster” are close but secondary—the immediate, visceral trigger is the *review itself* (the questionnaire, the back-and-forth, the delay).

### Trigger Moment + Exact Search Terms/Phrases Buyers Type
**Trigger moment:** The seller receives (or anticipates) a security questionnaire/RFP with an “AI/ML” or “generative AI” section, or a buyer’s security team flags the AI product as a blocker. This often happens mid-to-late sales cycle when the deal is otherwise ready but stalls on compliance. It can also occur when prepping a Trust Center or responding to repeated customer asks for “evidence our AI is secure.”

**Exact search phrases buyers type (based on real 2026 patterns for similar tools):**
- “AI security questionnaire automation”
- “automate AI security review” or “AI ML security questionnaire answers”
- “signed verifiable AI security evidence” or “AI model security attestation”
- “pass enterprise AI security review faster”
- “security questionnaire AI section” / “generative AI vendor assessment”
- “trust center AI security evidence”

These mirror how teams search for Vanta Questionnaire Automation, Conveyor, etc.

### What Makes It Sell Off-the-Shelf (5-Second “Yes, That’s My Problem” Homepage)
The winning homepage instantly matches the buyer’s exact pain with ultra-specific, benefit-first copy and a frictionless free trial:
- Headline: “Signed, verifiable security evidence for the AI you ship. Clear enterprise reviews in days, not weeks.”
- Subhead / hero: “Free AI security scan — upload your model or repo and get signed attestations buyers actually accept.”
- Prominent CTA: Big “Try free scan” or “Upload model for instant evidence” button (no form wall).
- Supporting proof: “Used by teams closing [X] enterprise deals/week” or logos + “Replaces weeks of back-and-forth on AI questionnaires.”
- Trust signals: Examples of the signed output (e.g., “Verifiable attestation PDF with cryptographic proof”) and quick wins (“Answer AI sections 10x faster”).

This mirrors how top tools convert self-serve: visitors recognize their exact stalled-deal scenario and immediately see a low-risk way to test.

### Real 2026 Examples That Nail This Positioning (Self-Serve “Pass the Security Review / Answer the Questionnaire”)
These products dominate with homepage copy, free trials/scans, and workflows centered on rapid questionnaire response or trust-center self-service:

- **Vanta Questionnaire Automation** — https://www.vanta.com/products/questionnaire-automation  
  “Speed up security reviews with AI-powered automation. Streamline security reviews with agentic workflows that automate questionnaires from start to finish. Vanta takes care of the heavy lifting—so all you need to do is review, approve, and submit.” They emphasize 5x faster responses and a knowledge base for consistent, review-ready answers.[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/products/questionnaire-automation)

- **Conveyor** — https://www.conveyor.com/  
  Positions as “AI for Security Questionnaires, Trust Center & RFPs.” Strong focus on connecting AI to source material for accurate, low-maintenance questionnaire automation and self-serve trust centers that let buyers access evidence without back-and-forth.[[2]](https://www.conveyor.com/)

- **SafeBase-powered Trust Centers** (widely adopted, e.g., Drata, OpenAI, Wiz, Confluent) — Examples: https://trust.drata.com/, https://trust.openai.com/, https://trust.wiz.io/  
  “Start your security review” + self-serve access to artifacts, reports, and gated documents. Many vendors use SafeBase to power proactive trust centers that reduce questionnaire volume by letting buyers self-serve evidence.[[3]](https://trust.drata.com/)

These succeed because they speak directly to the questionnaire/review pain, offer instant value (automation or self-serve portals), and convert via free/low-friction entry points. Kolm’s verifiable/signed AI-specific angle can differentiate by targeting the emerging “AI section” blocker that generic tools handle less precisely.
